data.title: Stock index futures fall as geopolitical, trade tensions weigh; focus on PPI report
data.description:
Stock index futures fell on Thursday, as geopolitics and trade tensions stayed in the limelight, while investors awaited the wholesale inflation data. 

S&P 500 futures (SPX) -0.5%, Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) -0.6%, and Dow futures (INDU) -0.5%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) fell 1 basis point to 4.41%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) fell 1 basis point to 3.94%.

Wall Street's major averages gave up gains in the afternoon to turn lower, eventually ending in the red amid a lack of details on trade developments. Gains sparked by a strong $39B 10-year note auction also sputtered out.

"For equities, the renewed geopolitical risks outweighed the good news on CPI, with the S&P 500 ending a run of three consecutive increases," Deutsche Bank's Jim Reid said. 

President Donald Trump said he intended to send letters to trading partners in the next week or two setting unilateral tariff rates, ahead of a July 9 deadline to reimpose higher duties on dozens of countries.

"Trump said they would send letters to U.S. trading partners, detailing the threatened 9 July unilateral trade taxes. Market reactions have been a muted negative; investors have become conditioned to expect retreats from such threats. However, the uncertainty created may weigh on US investment and hiring in the near term," UBS' Paul Donovan said.

Traders also continued to await any details on the U.S.-China trade deal. 

On geopolitical news, the U.S. government on Wednesday ordered the departure of embassy staff from Baghdad after Iran threatened to attack U.S. bases if talks over its nuclear program fall through.

"Sentiment wasn’t helped by various headlines, and CBS News reported that part of the reason for the U.S. moving staff out of the Middle East was because U.S. officials had been told that Israel was ready to launch an operation into Iran," Reid said.

Another set of inflation data will land before the bell on Thursday. The May core producer price index is expected to come in at 0.3% M/M and 3.1% Y/Y.

PPI is forecasted to come in at 2.6% on an annual basis and 0.2% M/M.

The initial jobless claims report will also be released at the same time and is expected to fall to 242K.

The Fed balance sheet will come in later in the day.

data.title: European indexes mostly lower amid trade uncertainties, UK trade deficit widens
data.description:
London (UKX) +0.06% to 8,869, UK's trade deficit widens in April. Industrial production in the UK declined by 0.6% M/M in April. The British pound hovered around $1.35, near its highest level in three years, supported by broad-based dollar weakness.

Germany (DAX:IND) -1.06% to 23,698, 

France (CAC:IND) -0.69% to 7,722.

Finland's current account deficit narrowed sharply to €222M in April.

The annual inflation rate in the Netherlands stood at 3.3% in May.

The pan-European Stoxx 600 (STOXX) dropped 0.61% to 548.2, and almost all sectors in the red led by travel. Investor sentiment gripped by trade uncertainties as President Donald announced plans to send letters to key trading partners within the next one to two weeks. 

Coming up in the session: A lighter day on the charts today.

In the bond market, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was down less than 1 basis point to 4.40%.

Germany's 10-year yield was down 4 basis points to 2.50%.

UK's 10-year yield was down 4 basis points to 4.52%. 

Currencies: (EUR:USD) (GBP:USD) (CHF:USD)

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWG), (NYSE:GF), (NYSEARCA:EWI), (NYSEARCA:EWQ), (NASDAQ:FGM), (NASDAQ:DAX), (NYSEARCA:FLGR), (NYSEARCA:FXB), (NYSEARCA:EWU), (NASDAQ:FKU), (BATS:EWUS), (NYSEARCA:FLGB), (NYSEARCA:GREK)

data.title: Hexagon AB acquires France's APEI to enhance aerial mapping and geospatial platforms
data.description:
Hexagon AB (OTCPK:HXGBY) has announced an agreement to acquire France-based APEI, specializing in aerial mapping, to bolster geospatial data offerings.

The acquisition will strengthen Hexagon's capabilities in providing accurate geospatial data.

APEI specializes in using private aircraft for aerial data collection in France, Europe, and Africa. They have been a long-term Hexagon partner, contributing to the Hexagon Content Program, the largest aerial imagery library in the US and Europe, and a core application on HxDR's cloud-based platform.

 APEI's team of about 10 is anticipated to bring in approximately €3 million in 2025 revenue, and will join Hexagon's Geosystems division.

The deal is expected to be finalized by the end of Q2 2025.

data.title: Ericsson and Google Cloud partner to launch AI-powered, carrier-grade 5G core-as-a-service
data.description:
• Ericsson (NASDAQ:ERIC) Thursday reported the launch of Ericsson On-Demand, a new solution delivering core network services as a true software-as-a-service (SaaS) platform to communications service providers (CSPs).
  • The platform is designed with Google Cloud, leveraging AI infrastructure and Google Kubernetes Engine (GKE) - and is managed end-to-end by Ericsson.
  • The solution will help CSPs to quickly set up and grow core network services, cut operating costs, and gain business flexibility with a fully managed, cloud-native platform, the companies said.

  • Muninder Singh Sambi, vice president and general manager of Networking and Security, Google Cloud, said: "With Ericsson On-Demand on Google Cloud's AI infrastructure, CSPs can rapidly deploy 5G core and tap into new revenue streams."

  • Source: Press Release

data.title: China to place six-month limit on rare earth export licenses for U.S. firms - report
data.description:
China will place a six-month limit on rare earth export licenses granted to U.S. automakers and other manufacturers, people familiar with the matter told The Wall Street Journal, which would give Beijing leverage if tensions with the U.S. escalate again.

China is expected to start approving U.S. companies' license applications soon, possibly within a week of President Donald Trump and China's Xi Jinping signing off on the trade framework reached in London this week.

The temporary licenses will largely involve rare earths used in manufacturing electric vehicles, consumer electronics, military equipment, and wind turbines.

Once China approves the applications, the U.S. will relax some restrictions on sales of jet engines and related parts, as well as ethane, to China.

On Wednesday, Trump said the trade deal with China was done, subject to final approval by him and Xi. "Full magnets, and any necessary rare earths, will be supplied, up front, by China," he added.

Rare earths have become a major bargaining chip for China in negotiations, as the country accounts for nearly 70% of global production and more than 90% of processing. Restrictions on rare earths have already impacted the global automotive industry, raising concerns of supply shortages.

data.title: Tutor Perini subsidiary secures $67M contract
data.description:
• Tutor Perini (NYSE:TPC) subsidiary, Lunda Construction Company, has been awarded a contract by the Minnesota Department of Transportation valued at approximately $67.2M for the I-94 and I-394 Interchange Project in Minneapolis.
  • The scope of work includes repairing 34 bridges and ramps along I-394 and I-94 between downtown Minneapolis and Highway 100.
  • Work is expected to begin in July 2025 with substantial completion anticipated in the summer of 2027.
  • The contract value has been added to the company’s backlog in Q2 2025.

data.title: Ivanhoe restarts part of Kakula copper mine in Congo
data.description:
• Ivanhoe Mines on Wednesday announced the restart of underground mining operations on the Western Side of Kakula Mine on June 7.
  • Mining activities on the eastern side of the Kakula Mine are expected to start imminently, with activities solely focused on developing access drives to a new mining area east of the existing mine workings.
  • Underground water levels in Kakula Mine stabilized; dewatering to commence in Q3 2025.
  • Kamoa-Kakula revises 2025 production guidance to between 370,000 and 420,000 tonnes of copper.
  • Smelter start-up expected to commence in September 2025, with first anode anticipated in October 2025.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Chime Financial prices IPO at $27, above expected band, raising $864M
data.description:
San Francisco–based financial technology company Chime (CHYM) priced its initial public offering of 32M shares at $27.00 per share, above its previously marketed range of $24.00-$26.00 band.

The offering consists of 25,900,765 shares of Class A common stock to be sold by Chime and ~6.1M shares of Class A common stock to be sold by existing stockholders.

The offering is expected to raise ~$864 million for Chime, based on the $27 per share price.

Underwriters have a 30-day option to purchase up to an additional 4.8M shares.

The shares are expected to begin trading on the Nasdaq Global Select Market on June 12, 2025, under the ticker symbol “CHYM.”

The offering is expected to close on June 13, 2025.

data.title: Intensify Therapeutics slumps 27%, prices $2M stock offering
